meta.description: EffortOS — Sports activity tracker & analytics. Upload TCX/GPX files, compute training metrics, and get personalized coaching advice.

landing.title: EffortOS - Sports Activity Tracker & Analytics
landing.meta_description: EffortOS is a sports activity tracking and analytics platform. Upload TCX/GPX files to auto-calculate TSS, CTL, ATL and other training metrics. AI coach provides personalized training advice.
landing.og_description: Upload activity data, smart training analytics, AI-powered coaching advice
landing.hero_desc: Upload TCX/GPX activity files to auto-calculate TSS, CTL, ATL and other training metrics. Science-based training load analysis with AI-powered personalized coaching.
landing.feature_metrics_desc: TSS, CTL, ATL, TSB, NP, IF and more — auto-calculated
landing.feature_charts_desc: PMC trends, training calendar, HR/power zone charts
landing.feature_ai_desc: Weekly reports and personalized training advice powered by AI

login.invalid_email: Please enter a valid email address
login.dev_console_hint: Check the console in development mode

ai.title: AI Coach
ai.report_desc: Based on the last 7 days of training data and 42-day historical load, generate a training report and 7-day plan.
ai.request_failed_retry: Request failed, please try again later
ai.question_placeholder: Enter your question (optional), e.g.: Should I do a recovery ride or intervals this week?

activities.title: Activities
activities.dropzone_text: Click or drag files here (multiple supported)
activities.select_one_file: Please select at least one file

activity_detail.title: Activity Detail
activity_detail.tss_tooltip: Training Stress Score — measures the training load of this activity. Click the value to edit manually.
activity_detail.hr_tss_tooltip: Heart Rate Training Stress Score = Duration(h) × (avgHR/LTHR)² × 100
activity_detail.np_if_tooltip: NP: Normalized Power, accounts for variability. IF: Intensity Factor = NP/FTP
activity_detail.work_vi_ef_tooltip: Work: Total energy. VI: Variability Index = NP/avgPower. EF: Efficiency Factor = NP/avgHR
activity_detail.no_timeseries: No time-series data for this activity (HR, power, speed, etc.)
activity_detail.upload_hint: Upload a TCX/GPX file with sensor data to view detailed charts
activity_detail.power_curve_title: Power Curve (This Activity vs. Personal Best)
activity_detail.avg_pace_hrif_tooltip: Average pace: total distance / total time (min:sec/km). HR IF: Heart Rate Intensity Factor = avg HR / LTHR
activity_detail.running_ef_tooltip: Running EF = avg speed (m/s) / avg HR (bpm), measures running speed efficiency at a given heart rate. Higher is better.

settings.title: Settings
settings.params_desc: Set FTP, threshold heart rate, and max heart rate for calculating TSS, zones, and other training metrics. Changes will trigger automatic recalculation from the effective date.
settings.no_params: No parameters set yet. Please fill in the form above and save.
settings.preview_after_save: Preview will appear after saving parameters
settings.preview_after_ftp: Preview will appear after saving FTP
settings.confirm_delete: Delete this parameter record? Affected activities will be recalculated.

about.title: About EffortOS
about.subtitle: Science-based training, data-driven, AI-powered
about.desc1: EffortOS is a sports activity tracking and analytics tool for amateur and semi-pro athletes, similar to intervals.icu. Upload TCX/GPX files to auto-calculate TSS, CTL, ATL, TSB and other core training metrics, with AI-powered personalized coaching advice.
about.core_philosophy: Core philosophy: Quantify training load with science — train smarter, not harder.
about.feature_metrics_desc: TSS, CTL/ATL/TSB, NP, IF, EF, power curve, Best Efforts — auto-calculated
about.feature_charts_desc: PMC trends, training calendar heatmap, fitness trends, HR/power zone charts, activity comparison
about.feature_ai_desc: AI-powered weekly reports and personalized advice using OpenAI GPT
about.feature_wellness_desc: Daily subjective feelings + HRV/resting HR logging, auto-calculated Readiness score
about.feature_export_desc: Export activity data in CSV or JSON format with multi-dimensional filtering
about.open_source_desc: EffortOS is also an AI self-driving experiment: powered by Claude Code, it continuously runs a cycle of planning, task selection, implementation, evaluation, and re-planning — evolving autonomously with only high-level goals and no detailed design.

help.title: User Guide
help.step1: Set sport parameters — Go to Settings and fill in FTP (cycling), LTHR, and max heart rate
help.step2: Upload activity data — Upload TCX/GPX files on the Activities page; sport type is auto-detected
help.step3: View training metrics — Dashboard and activity detail pages show training load analysis
help.step4: Get AI advice — AI Coach generates personalized advice based on your training data
help.tss_desc: Training Stress Score. Measures the training load of a single workout. 100 TSS ≈ 1 hour at threshold intensity. Calculated from power (when FTP is set) or heart rate.
help.ctl_desc: Chronic Training Load. 42-day exponentially weighted moving average of TSS, reflecting long-term fitness. Higher CTL means greater recent training volume and stronger fitness base.
help.atl_desc: Acute Training Load. 7-day exponentially weighted moving average of TSS, reflecting short-term fatigue. ATL rises quickly after training.
help.tsb_desc: Training Stress Balance = CTL - ATL. Positive values indicate good recovery (suitable for racing); negative values indicate fatigue accumulation (training phase). -10 to +10 is optimal for racing.
help.np_desc: Normalized Power. Accounts for acceleration, deceleration, and intervals to produce an equivalent steady-state power. Better reflects true intensity than average power.
help.if_desc: Intensity Factor = NP / FTP. Reflects how hard this activity was relative to your threshold power. IF > 1.0 means above threshold.
help.vi_desc: Variability Index = NP / Average Power. VI = 1.0 means perfectly steady pace (e.g., time trial). Higher values indicate more variable pacing.
help.ef_desc: Efficiency Factor = NP / Average Heart Rate. Measures power output efficiency at a given heart rate. EF increases with training and is a good indicator of fitness progress.
help.intensity_level_desc: Auto-rated based on Intensity Factor (IF): Recovery (<0.65), Endurance (<0.80), Tempo (<0.90), Threshold (<1.05), VO2max (≥1.05).
help.hr_zones_desc: Auto-calculated based on LTHR. Configure LTHR for each sport type in Settings.
help.pw_zones_desc: Auto-calculated based on FTP. Configure cycling FTP in Settings.
help.tcx_desc: Garmin Training Center XML format. Contains heart rate, power, speed, cadence, elevation and other full time-series data.
help.gpx_desc: GPS Exchange Format. Supports Garmin extensions (heart rate, power, cadence). GPS track + extended metrics.
help.best_efforts_desc: The system auto-calculates peak power and heart rate for time windows from 5 seconds to 60 minutes. Gold PB badges in the activity list indicate new personal bests.
help.compare_desc: Select two activities for side-by-side comparison of metrics and trends. Go to the Activity Comparison page.
help.weekly_report_desc: The 'AI Training Report' button on the dashboard generates a weekly report. AI Coach analyzes training data, best efforts, and intensity distribution for personalized insights. You can also ask questions on the AI Coach page.
help.power_curve_desc: Activity detail page shows a power curve comparison: blue fill is historical peak power/HR, orange dashed is current activity. See how your performance compares to your personal best.
help.fitness_trend_desc: Dashboard provides two trend charts: NP / Efficiency Factor (EF) dual-axis line chart tracks power efficiency; Training Intensity Distribution shows TSS vs IF relationship. Track your fitness progress over time.
help.export_desc: Activity list page supports exporting data in CSV or JSON format for analysis in other tools. Filter by sport type, date range, and intensity level before exporting.
help.search_sort_desc: Activity list supports keyword search by name, and sorting by date, upload time, or name.
help.batch_ops_desc: Select multiple activities in the list to batch delete.
